results.rendered_html:
āyata p. p.
1 Long; daśāṃgulamāyatā yaṣṭiḥ a stick ten fingers long; śatamadhyardhaṃ (yojanaṃ) āyatā  Mb. ; ˚svabhāvāni ca duḥkhāni  K. 175  lasting.
2 Diffuse, prolix.
3 Big, large, great;  Pt. 1. 
4 Drawn, attracted.
5 Longdrawn, distant.
6 Curbed, restrained.
— taḥ An oblong (in geometry).
— taṃ ind. Deeply, long; as in āyataṃ niśvasya.
Comp.
— akṣa a. (kṣī f.),
— īkṣaṇa, -netra, -locana a. (a woman) with large eyes.
— apāṃga a. having long-cornered eyes.
— ardhaḥ half an oblong.
— āyatiḥ f. long continuance, remote futurity;  Śi. 14. 5. 
— chadā a plantain tree.
— lekha a. long-curved;  Ku. 1. 47. 
— stūḥ m. [āyataṃ stauti, ni˚ dīrghaḥ] a panegyrist, bard.
